tldr: About half your live ads are running the same handful of headlines and body lines word-for-word, and several have been up for three to four months without a break. Meta sees these as basically the same ad, so it quietly picks one and stops showing the rest, meaning you paid to make ads almost no one is seeing.

benchmark.context: The typical brand in your space swaps in new ads about every 21 days, and the best ones do it every 14. A few of your ads have been running for 90 to 126 days, which is way past the point where they still pull their weight.

ads.headline: Turn Your Ruler into a Cutting Machine!
ads.body: Turn Your Ruler into a Cutting Machine, Even for 1/4" & 7/8" Sizes
drivers: This ad has been up for 126 days, which is six times longer than your typical brand keeps an ad fresh.
The exact same headline is running on three other live ads of yours, so Meta treats them as one ad and only shows a slice of what you're paying for.
The headline repeats itself in the body line, so a shopper who reads both sees the same words twice and learns nothing new.

ads.headline: What's the Grip Strip Buzz?
ads.body: Quilters Are Raving About Grip Strips. See What the Buzz Is About!
drivers: 126 days live with no fresh angle means the quilters who were going to click already have, and everyone else is scrolling past.
"See what the buzz is about" doesn't tell a shopper what a Grip Strip actually does, so people who don't already know your brand have no reason to stop.
A near-identical version of this ad just launched 2 days ago, so the two are competing for the same eyeballs and splitting whatever attention is left.

ads.headline: Turn Your Ruler into a Cutting Machine!
ads.body: Zero Fabric Waste Starts Here. Cut with Confidence, Even at 2 7/8"
drivers: Three of your live videos use this exact headline and this exact body copy, which is the biggest single reason Meta is quietly holding your budget back.
91 days running is more than four times your normal ad lifespan, so the video has almost certainly stopped earning its slot.
"Even at 2 7/8"" is a technical detail that means something to a seasoned quilter but stops a new shopper cold.

ads.headline: Guidelines Ruler
ads.body: Fabric waste has you down? 😱 Introducing our revolutionary Guidelines Ruler! 💡 Non-Slip, Connectable, Unbreakable, and Left-Handed Friendly Click Learn More below.#NoMoreFabricWaste #AccurateQuiltPieceCutting
drivers: The headline is just the product name, so a shopper scrolling by has no reason to slow down and read the rest.
This exact body copy is copied word-for-word on another live ad of yours, so you're paying to make two ads Meta sees as one.
108 days of the same wording means anyone in your quilting audience who was going to buy from this line has already seen it many times over.

ads.headline: Super Easy to Hold
ads.body: Sick of fabric waste? 🤢 Say goodbye to slipping and eyeballing with our revolutionary cutting solution.😍 Use with the 12" & 24" rulers you have and start saving time,موقع طليعة كوم start saving time, money & hassle! Click Shop Now.
drivers: 108 days live puts this ad five times past the point where it still pays for itself.
An almost identical version of this body copy is running on another ad, so Meta bundles them together and shows less of both.
"Super Easy to Hold" as the headline gives no hint what the product does or who it's for, so it does none of the work of stopping the scroll.

ads_compact.headline: Simpler Way to Make Triangles
ads_compact.body: No-Guesswork Hexagons. Tap "Learn More" for tips on mastering sharp mitered corners and making accurate flying geese and diamond-in-a-square blocks.

ads_compact.headline: Step-By-Step Instructions
ads_compact.body: Pick a Block, Any Block & Make It Any Size. No Math. No Dies. Click or tap the learn more button.

ads_compact.headline: Make Quilt Blocks Any Size
ads_compact.body: Make Half Square Triangles Any Size. No Extra Trimming Needed. No Math, No Fabric Waste, No Expensive Templates or Dies!

next_step.urgency: Four of your ads are on track to stop working within the next week or two, and the ruler and scant quarter-inch groups are the first to go.
next_step.headline: Walk through the Guidelines for Quilting refresh plan with Kyle
next_step.body:
John, in this 30 min call, we'll map out:
• 3 customer avatars at your price point
• The motivational hook driving each
• A 30-day creative + spend roadmap (to outbeat your current ROAS)

Yours to keep - work with us or don't.

method_note: Based only on your public Facebook and Instagram ads, the pictures, the wording, and how long each one has been live. No access to your account or private numbers.
